creator: gary@palmer.demon.co.uk
text:
-current kernel of about 7 hours ago seems to have a problem setting the
MTU for a SLIP line. My network provider recommmends a MTU for SLIP of 536
(for various reasons to do with their internal network), and trying to
auto-set the MTU to that value in /etc/hostname.sl0 causes a kernel panic
(page fault). Trying from the command line after booting has similar
results.

Fix: 

Unknown.
How-To-Repeat: 
ifconfig sl0 inet hostname gateway mtu 536
